# Test: Complete E2E Release Workflow
# This test validates the complete schema release workflow from app repository
# creation through canonical repository release and dependency consumption.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Initialize canonical repository
mkdir canonical-repo
cd canonical-repo
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=test-canonical --skip-git --non-interactive

# Verify canonical structure
exists buf.yaml
exists catalog/Dockerfile
exists CODEOWNERS

# Create common schema
exec mkdir -p proto/common/v1
cp ../common.proto proto/common/v1/common.proto
exists proto/common/v1/common.proto

# Commit and tag
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial canonical schemas'
exec git tag v1.0.0
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Initial canonical schemas'
cd ..

# Step 2: Initialize first app repository (payment service)
mkdir payment-app
cd payment-app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/payments/ledger/v1

# Verify app structure
exists apx.yaml
exists internal/apis/proto/payments/ledger/v1/ledger.proto

# Commit
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Add payment service with ledger API'
exec git tag proto/payments/ledger/v1/v1.0.0
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Add payment service'
cd ..

# Step 3: Test dependency management
cd payment-app
exec apx add proto/common/v1@v1.0.0
exists apx.lock
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.lock
exec grep 'v1.0.0' apx.lock

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Add common dependency'
cd ..

# Step 4: Initialize second app repository (user service)
mkdir user-app
cd user-app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/users/profile/v1

# Verify user service structure
exists apx.yaml
exists internal/apis/proto/users/profile/v1/profile.proto

# Add dependency on common
exec apx add proto/common/v1@v1.0.0
exists apx.lock
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.lock

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Add user service with profile API'
exec git tag proto/users/profile/v1/v1.0.0
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Add user service'
cd ..

# Step 5: Test release prepare dry-run from payment app
cd payment-app
exec apx release prepare proto/payments/ledger/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/test-canonical --version=v1.0.0 --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'
cd ..

# Step 6: Validate all repos have correct structure
exists canonical-repo/buf.yaml
exists canonical-repo/proto/common/v1/common.proto
exists payment-app/apx.yaml
exists payment-app/internal/apis/proto/payments/ledger/v1/ledger.proto
exists user-app/apx.yaml
exists user-app/internal/apis/proto/users/profile/v1/profile.proto

# Step 7: Verify git history across all repos
cd canonical-repo
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Initial canonical schemas'
cd ..

cd payment-app
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Add payment service'
cd ..

cd user-app
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Add user service'
cd ..

# Success: Complete workflow validated
# - Canonical repository initialized with common schemas
# - App repositories created with module structure
# - Dependencies added and locked
# - Code generation with overlays works
# - Release prepare dry-run succeeds
# - Git history preserved in all repos

-- common.proto --
syntax = "proto3";

package common.v1;

option go_package = "github.com/testorg/test-canonical/gen/go/common/v1;commonv1";

message Money {
  string currency = 1;
  int64 amount = 2;
}

message Address {
  string street = 1;
  string city = 2;
  string state = 3;
  string postal_code = 4;
  string country = 5;
}
